records.ety: [L. contusus, p. p. of contundere to beat, crush; con- + tundere to beat, akin to Skr. tud (for stud) to strike, Goth. stautan. See Stutter.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Contused (kn*tūzd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Contusing.]

defs: To beat, pound, or bray together.
quotes: Roots, barks, and seeds contused together.

defs: To bruise; to injure or disorganize a part without breaking the skin.
